Home → Demo Hub

Read-only demo package

Stakeholder Demo Hub - The Agent Command Center

A premium browser-viewable demo hub for the production-visible readiness architecture. This package is verified through MVP-50, keeps runtime activation disabled, and turns the Markdown source package into a guided stakeholder experience. It is the stakeholder-facing bridge from the Command Center Launchpad into the rest of the demo.

Production verified through MVP-50 NOT_READY_FOR_REAL_AUTOMATION Runtime activation not started Read-only static HTML
Launch Live Dashboard Open Presentation Mode
Readiness layers 8 MVP-43 through MVP-50
Viewable pages 14 Including the checklist page
Production reports 67 Repo-derived verification reports
Validators 280 Python validator scripts
Agent count 47,979 Derived from `09_exports/org_chart_export.json`
Department count 1,777 Derived from `09_exports/org_chart_export.json`

Safety boundary

This is a viewable static demo package. It does not enable runtime behavior, command execution, action execution, writes, automation, or rollback.

Guided demo

What to open first

Use these pages for the stakeholder walk-through. Each page is static HTML and stays inside the Netlify publish directory.

Stakeholder Presentation

The 12-slide storyline, rendered as browser-readable sections for executive review and screen-share pacing.

Open

Command Center Sandbox Simulator

A safe, interactive runnable static simulator that walks a request through Auth, Storage, Audit, Approval, Dry Run, Queue, Human Review, and Monitoring Readiness at ./simulator.html.

Open

Runtime Foundation

Runtime planning track - not live execution. Review the controls, schemas, contracts, and checklists required before live runtime activation can ever be considered.

Open Runtime Foundation

Runtime Kernel

Submit a real persisted runtime request, create audit events, and move an item through approval status. Approval does not execute actions.

Open Runtime Kernel

Runtime Agent Control

Supervised single-agent test only. Activate or deactivate one controlled runtime agent with audit logging and a visible kill switch.

Open Runtime Agent Control

10-Agent Runtime Squad

Activate, deactivate, heartbeat, and log readiness notes for a controlled 10-agent supervised squad. No command execution.

Open 10-Agent Runtime Squad

100-Agent Runtime Battalion

Activate, deactivate, heartbeat, and monitor a controlled 100-agent supervised runtime battalion across 10 lanes. No command execution.

Open 100-Agent Runtime Battalion

500-Agent Runtime Group

Activate, deactivate, heartbeat, and monitor a controlled 500-agent supervised runtime group across 50 lanes. No command execution.

Open 500-Agent Runtime Group

250-Agent Runtime Company

Activate, deactivate, heartbeat, and monitor a controlled 250-agent supervised runtime company across 25 lanes. No command execution.

Open 250-Agent Runtime Company

1,000-Agent Runtime Division

Activate, deactivate, heartbeat, and monitor a controlled 1,000-agent supervised runtime division across 10 subdivisions and 100 lanes. No command execution.

Open 1,000-Agent Runtime Division

Department-Gated Runtime Expansion

Approve department gates and activate supervised runtime capacity by department. Global cap: 2,500. No command execution.

Open Department-Gated Runtime Expansion

5,000-Agent Runtime Corps

Scale supervised runtime capacity up to a 5,000-agent cap through approved department gates only. No raw fleet activation or command execution.

Open 5,000-Agent Runtime Corps

20,000-Agent Runtime Army

Scale supervised runtime capacity toward a 20,000-agent cap through approved department gates, staged unlocks, and circuit breakers. No raw fleet activation or command execution.

Open 20,000-Agent Runtime Army

Full 47,979-Agent Runtime Fleet

Full-fleet runtime capacity through approved department gates, staged unlocks, circuit breakers, and kill-switch controls. No raw activate all route or command execution.

Open Full 47,979-Agent Runtime Fleet

Full-Fleet Load Test

Stress-test the 47,979-agent control system with simulated load, failure scenarios, circuit breakers, recovery drills, and audit proof. No destructive execution.

Open Full-Fleet Load Test

Full-Fleet Observability Command Wall

One command-wall view for fleet scale, department gates, stages, cohorts, circuit breakers, load tests, recovery drills, Continual Harness status, and audit health.

Open Full-Fleet Observability Command Wall

Executive Control Room

A role-aware executive view for reviewing the full 47,979-agent command system, safety boundaries, observability, load tests, recovery drills, and next decisions.

Open Executive Control Room

Enterprise Pilot Room

A pilot-ready review room with scope, stakeholder roles, success criteria, risk register, safety boundaries, legal links, and go/no-go decision support.

Open Enterprise Pilot Room

1,777-Department Runtime Map

Map all 1,777 departments to supervised runtime readiness, lane coverage, and audit status without activating the full 47,979-agent registry.

Open 1,777-Department Runtime Map

System Story

Plain-English explanation of the command center, why readiness matters, and why runtime remains disabled.

Open

System Scale

Repo-derived counts with exact declared totals for the public registry and scale summary.

Open

Agent / Department Hierarchy

The seven-level hierarchy, the 11 operational domains, and the canonical registry that now exposes exact counts.

Open

Agent Registry

Source-traceable exact counts, family breakdowns, and the publishable registry view.

Open

Operating Model

The request flow from identity through audit, approval, dry-run, queueing, human review, and go / no-go.

Open

Validator and Safety Gate Map

The flat E2E validator pattern, the master wall, and the specific safety markers that keep runtime off.

Open

Safety Boundaries

A compact readout of the runtime capabilities that remain disabled.

Open

Technical Appendix

Validator strategy, flat E2E design, known limitations, and future runtime activation gates.

Open

Objections / FAQ

Twenty-plus stakeholder questions answered directly and without hype.

Open

Review / Scorecard

Executive questions, technical checks, safety criteria, and the demo success scorecard.

Open

Review QA Checklist

Seven-persona review readiness checklist for the final stakeholder pass.

Open

System scale snapshot

Verified counts from the repo

Every exact count below comes from manifests, file counts, dashboard data, or the canonical export graph. Exact agent and department counts are now declared from project artifacts.

Readiness layers

8 layers, verified through MVP-50.

Production verification reports

67 repo-derived reports.

Product-track files

582 files in `09_exports/mvp_product_track`.

Release artifacts

177 files in `09_exports/release_package`.

UI model JSON files

226 files in `14_backend/product_runtime/ui_models`.

Validator scripts

280 `validate_*.py` scripts in `scripts/`.

Dashboard static artifacts

70 files in `13_web_dashboard/dist`.

Stakeholder package files

19 files in the stakeholder presentation package.

Demo package files

16 files in the external demo package.

Viewable demo pages

12 HTML pages in this hub.

Operational domains

11 documented domains instead of a fake department registry.

Agent registry

47,979 team-member agents and 1,777 departments derived from `org_chart_export.json`.

Disabled capability categories

13 categories in the presentation manifest.

System at a Glance

How the demo hangs together

The diagrams are text-first and intentionally simple so the architecture stays readable on a projector or a phone.

Registered agent registry

  1. Registered Agent Registry
  2. Departments
  3. Units
  4. Families
  5. Operational Domains
  6. Safety Gates
  7. Static Simulator
  8. Human Review

Readiness flow

  1. Request
  2. Auth
  3. Storage
  4. Audit
  5. Approval
  6. Dry Run
  7. Queue
  8. Human Review
  9. Monitoring Readiness

Registered vs Runtime

  • Registered agents: 47,979
  • Live runtime agents: 0
  • Activation: not started
  • Meaning: the registry exists; automation remains disabled.

11 Operational Domains

Demo-facing operating domain map

Source type: demo-facing operating domain map derived from MVP-43 through MVP-50 readiness architecture.

  • Authentication / identity readiness
  • Request storage readiness
  • Audit ledger readiness
  • Approval gate readiness
  • Dry-run readiness
  • Controlled action queue readiness
  • Human-approved execution readiness
  • Monitoring / rollback / incident readiness
  • Demo / stakeholder review readiness
  • Validator / quality gate readiness
  • Safety boundary readiness

Glossary

Plain-English translation for the demo terms

Registered agent

A registered command-center operating unit listed in the static org-chart registry.

Live runtime agent

A live autonomous process. The count is 0 in this build.

Runtime activation

Turning on live execution. It has not started.

Static simulator

A browser-only in-memory demo that does not call backends or execute actions.

Backend/Supabase readiness

The architecture is prepared, but live writes and public mutation remain disabled.

Validator

A script that checks files, markers, and safety conditions.

Safety gate

A hard stop that prevents unsafe movement into runtime or mutation flows.

MVP-50

The current verified demo milestone, summarized here as MVP-50 Production Verified.

At a glance

What this hub says and does not say

What it says

  • Production verified through MVP-50.
  • Roadmap complete through MVP-50.
  • Runtime activation has not started.
  • Validators, safety gates, and review materials are visible.
  • Exact agent count: 47,979.
  • Exact department count: 1,777.
  • Live runtime agents enabled: 0.
  • Runtime activation: not started.

What it does not say

  • No live automation.
  • No command execution.
  • No public writes, database writes, or Supabase writes.
  • No alert sending, rollback execution, incident mutation, or deploy controls.

Exact counts we refuse to fake

  • Exact agent count: 47,979.
  • Exact department count: 1,777.
  • Formal registries are published in `agent-registry.html` and the static data files.

© 2026 Devin O’Rourke. All rights reserved.

Agent Command Center, the demo pages, registry presentation, dashboard copy, visual layout, simulator materials, validator descriptions, and stakeholder presentation materials are protected project materials. No license, sale, transfer, or permission to reuse is granted by viewing this demo.

This site is a static, read-only demonstration. It does not enable live automation, command execution, database writes, Supabase mutations, deployment actions, rollback execution, alert sending, or runtime activation.

Third-party names, platforms, and services referenced for context remain the property of their respective owners.
